title: Palantir Buys Fancy That To Add Retail, Shopping Data To Its Analytics Platform
summary: Palantir has acquired Fancy That, a startup specializing in retail and shopping data analytics, to expand its platform beyond law enforcement, financial, and healthcare sectors. Fancy That's technology helps retailers optimize omnichannel strategies, inventory, and customer habits using machine learning and sensor technologies. This acquisition marks Palantir's fourth and second in the marketing/sales area, complementing the growing importance of omnichannel strategies in commerce.

title: TurboTax Resumes State Tax Filing Service Nationwide After Fraud Concerns
summary: TurboTax resumed its state tax filing services nationwide after temporarily pausing them due to fraud concerns. Intuit, the maker of TurboTax, stated that the fraud instances did not result from a security breach of its systems and that they are working with states and security expert Palantir. This suspension followed actions by states like Minnesota, Utah, and Alabama to halt processing returns submitted through third-party software due to suspicious activity.

title: Hershey and Palantir partner to form CPG consortium
summary: The Hershey Company and Palantir Technologies have formed a consortium for consumer packaged goods (CPG) manufacturers. This partnership aims to leverage Palantir's data analysis expertise to help CPG companies share and analyze data, stay ahead of emerging trends, and adjust business strategies according to consumer purchasing patterns. The initiative seeks to evolve the CPG business and better serve consumers in a dynamic industry landscape.

title: Palantir wins lawsuit over US Army data system
summary: A federal judge has ruled in favor of Palantir Technologies, Inc. in a lawsuit against the U.S. Army regarding a contract for a data system. This ruling allows Palantir to compete for a portion of the $6 billion Distributed Common Ground System-Army (DCGS-A) system. The company had sued the Army, alleging bias and non-compliance with rules regarding commercially available products.

title: Live Nation Headquarters Moving to NYC’s Meatpacking District
summary: Live Nation, the world's largest music promoter, is relocating its headquarters from Times Square to a 100,000-square-foot building in New York City's Meatpacking District. This move follows Live Nation's record-breaking third quarter with $3.21 billion in revenues, a 23% increase year-over-year. The new location places Live Nation among other major tech and retail companies in the increasingly gentrified neighborhood.

title: Peter Thiel’s Mithril Capital Raises $850 Million VC Fund
summary: Mithril Capital Management LLC, co-founded by Peter Thiel, has successfully raised approximately $850 million for its second venture capital fund. The firm is employing an unconventional strategy by spreading management fees over 12 years, which is two years longer than the typical VC fund structure.

title: The OFCCP Paradox: Government Actions Create a New Diversity Hiring Fallacy
summary: This article examines the paradox created by OFCCP's diversity hiring enforcement, particularly focusing on the Palantir settlement and Oracle lawsuit. It highlights how government actions and statistical analysis methods clash with existing hiring complexities, such as H1-B visas and the definition of a "qualified" applicant. The author suggests methods for companies to better comply with OFCCP regulations while navigating these challenges.

title: McDonald’s customers can now pay for their fast food with PayMaya
summary: More than 40 McDonald’s restaurants in the Philippines have partnered with PayMaya Philippines, a digital financial services arm of PLDT Inc.'s Voyager Innovations, to accept cashless payments. This collaboration enables customers to use Mastercard and Visa credit, debit, and prepaid cards, with plans to expand to QR payments in select McCafé branches and for online bookings. The move aims to keep McDonald's relevant in the evolving digital landscape by embracing cashless transactions.

title: JP Morgan reportedly had to oust a security chief backed by Palantir after executives found out he was spying on them
summary: J.P. Morgan Chase reportedly ousted its "special operations" head, Peter Cavicchia III, after discovering he was spying on senior executives. Supported by over 100 Palantir engineers, Cavicchia's team, initially tasked with monitoring dishonest traders, began collecting extensive data on top bank officials, leading to an internal scandal and his forced resignation in 2013.

title: Tech Companies Quietly Work With ICE as Border Crisis Continues
summary: Several prominent technology companies have active contracts with the United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), profiting from the ongoing border crisis. These companies, including Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Thomson Reuters, Microsoft, Motorola Solutions, and Palantir, have contracts totaling millions of dollars, often operating out of public view. While some companies condemned the administration's family separation policy, their contractual relationships with ICE continue.

title: Stock Market Turns Lower; Google Shares Tank As U.S. Prepares Antitrust Probe
summary: The stock market, particularly the Nasdaq, turned lower on Monday, with Google-parent Alphabet's shares falling amid news of an impending antitrust probe by the U.S. Justice Department. Investors were also closely watching Apple stock ahead of its Worldwide Developers Conference.

title: CLOU Stock Price | Global X Cloud Computing ETF
summary: This article provides information on the Global X Cloud Computing ETF (CLOU), including its ticker symbol, current stock price, and trade exchange (NASDAQ). It offers key statistics such as the expense ratio, market capitalization, and inception date, and includes recent news and analysis related to cloud computing stocks. The article also lists major holdings of the ETF.

title: Palantir’s big push into Canada
summary: Palantir Technologies is making a significant move into Canada, appointing former Canadian ambassador to the U.S., David MacNaughton, to head its operations. This expansion, particularly with an office in Ottawa, signals Palantir's intention to deepen ties with the Canadian government and expand its data analytics services, despite facing privacy concerns and a lack of profitability in its U.S. operations. The article explores the potential reasons behind this push, including improving its public image, accessing new talent, and tapping into Canada's emerging cybersecurity market, while also highlighting civil liberties concerns from advocates.

title: Ottawa’s AI ethics test didn’t consider Palantir’s controversial work with U.S. law enforcement
summary: A report reveals that Canada's AI ethics test for government projects did not account for Palantir Technologies' controversial work with U.S. law enforcement agencies. Despite Ottawa's stance as a leader in ethical AI, federal procurement rules prevented officials from considering Palantir's involvement with U.S. immigration authorities and police forces when evaluating its bid for Canadian government contracts. This raises concerns about public confidence in the ethical use of AI by the government, especially given Palantir's history of controversial contracts.

title: Tilray Closes Merger With Privateer
summary: Tilray Inc. has officially closed its merger with Privateer Holdings, a Seattle-based private equity firm. The deal, announced in June, gives Tilray control over releasing 75 million shares to Privateer's stockholders and is expected to provide Tilray with greater control and operating flexibility. This merger follows a busy year for Tilray, which also included acquisitions of Natura Natural Holdings and Manitoba Harvest.

title: Rocket Lab channels SpaceX-like rapid launch capability in July 4 Electron mission
summary: Rocket Lab is aiming for SpaceX-like rapid launch capabilities with its Electron rocket, targeting its shortest turnaround time between missions from the same launch pad. The company is preparing for its second launch in three weeks, the "Pics Or It Didn’t Happen" mission, carrying several small satellites for Earth-imaging. Rocket Lab is also expanding its launch infrastructure and exploring booster recovery technology to further enhance its rapid launch goals.

title: Peter Thiel's Palantir confidentially files for public listing
summary: Palantir, a big data analytics company founded by Peter Thiel, has confidentially filed paperwork with the SEC for a proposed public listing of Class A common stock. The listing is expected to occur after the SEC's review process, with the company looking to raise around $961 million. This announcement follows rumors of the listing and earlier valuations of the company around $20 billion.

title: Tech Moves: Zillow co-founder joins Palantir board; Hootsuite names new CEO; and more
summary: This article details significant personnel changes and appointments within the Pacific Northwest tech community and beyond. Key moves include Zillow co-founder Spencer Rascoff joining Palantir Technologies' board, Hootsuite appointing Tom Keiser as its new CEO, and various other executive changes at companies like Intellectual Ventures, Qumulo, Avalara, and Capitolis. The report covers a range of positions from COOs and CMOs to directors and product managers, highlighting a dynamic period of leadership adjustments in the technology sector.

title: Yamato Holdings Co., Ltd. and Palantir Technologies Inc. to Begin Work Accelerating Digital Transformation of the Logistics Industry in Japan
summary: Yamato Holdings Co., Ltd. and Palantir Technologies Inc. have announced a partnership to accelerate the digital transformation of the logistics industry in Japan. Yamato will deploy Palantir Foundry to enhance its internal operations, optimize resource allocation, and streamline the supply chain by integrating real-time parcel trucking data. This initiative is part of Yamato's "YAMATO NEXT100" plan, involving a 100 billion yen investment in digital technologies and an investment in Palantir.

title: For-profit colleges received $1B in federal aid
summary: For-profit colleges received over $1 billion from the CARES Act, a federal relief package intended for at-need institutions and students, despite some being owned by billion-dollar companies and having histories of controversy. This allocation was considered historic as the Department of Education typically excludes for-profit schools from federal funds. Community colleges, which serve a majority of low-income students, received proportionally less support, leading to concerns about the formula used for distribution and the overall impact on different educational sectors.

title: Koss Corp. suing major headphone brands, including Apple, Bose, for patent infringement
summary: Koss Corp. has filed five lawsuits against major headphone brands, including Apple, Bose, JLAB Audio, Skullcandy Inc., and Plantronics Inc., alleging infringement of its wireless headphone technology patents. These patents stem from Koss's "Striva" project in the mid-2000s, which aimed to develop "truly wireless headphones." Koss is seeking damages and a ruling of willful infringement, which could triple the award amount, though a specific figure has not been disclosed.

title: Palantir Alum Recalls 2020 All-Nighter That Won Over Tyson Foods After CTO Called Demo 'Impossible' During Pandemic Supply Chain Crisis
summary: A former Palantir executive, Ka Ling Wu, recounted how a 24-hour demo during the early COVID-19 pandemic led to a significant partnership with Tyson Foods. Despite initial skepticism from Tyson's CTO, Palantir successfully mapped Tyson's entire supply chain within five days, leading to millions in savings and contributing to Palantir's development of HyperAuto. Palantir shares have since surged 489% over the past 12 months.

title: Peter Thiel-backed workplace app Asana files for direct listing on NYSE
summary: Asana Inc, a workplace app backed by Peter Thiel and valued at $1.5 billion, announced its plan for a direct listing on the New York Stock Exchange. This move follows similar direct listings by Spotify and Slack and precedes another anticipated direct listing from Thiel-backed Palantir Technologies. Asana was founded by Facebook co-founder Dustin Moskovitz and Justin Rosenstein, with Moskovitz and Rosenstein retaining significant voting power.

title: Data-analysis giant Palantir is moving its headquarters to Denver
summary: Data-analysis giant Palantir is relocating its headquarters from Palo Alto to Denver, updated on their website and social media. The company, known for its controversial work, had previously discussed a potential move to Colorado. This move signifies a notable shift for the Bay Area tech firm.

title: How Palantir's relocation to Colorado can boost future eco-devo efforts
summary: Palantir's decision to move its headquarters to Denver, Colorado, without seeking incentives, is seen as a significant endorsement of Colorado's economic ecosystem. This move by the $20 billion Bay Area data-analysis firm is expected to boost future economic development efforts in the state. The article highlights that this relocation sends a strong statement about the attractiveness of Colorado for major tech companies.

title: What to Know About Palantir, the Latest Tech Company to File to Go Public
summary: Palantir Technologies, the previously secretive data-analytics company, has made public its regulatory filing for a long-awaited initial public offering (IPO). The article discusses what to know about the company as it prepares to go public. It covers the filing of the IPO and offers insights into the company's background.

title: Palantir plans to go public as it moves away from reliance on government work
summary: Palantir Technologies Inc., known for its controversial work with government agencies, is preparing to go public through a direct listing. The company attributes its revenue growth to the private sector, which now accounts for over half of its customers. Palantir also plans to move its headquarters from Palo Alto, Calif., to Denver.

title: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) to start trading tomorrow through direct listing
summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is set to begin trading tomorrow through a direct listing on the NYSE. The company, founded in 2003, specializes in data analytics for government and commercial sectors, reporting significant revenue growth and decreasing net losses in 2020. This article details Palantir's financials, direct listing method, future outlook, and market opportunities, ahead of its public debut.

summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. saw its shares fall 5% from its opening trade price on its first day as a public company, closing at $9.50 on the New York Stock Exchange. The data analytics company, co-founded by Peter Thiel, opted for a direct listing instead of an initial public offering. This debut ended 17 years of secrecy surrounding the company's operations.

title: Palantir to Commence Trading on NYSE on September 30, 2020
summary: Palantir announced that its shares will begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) on September 30, 2020. This marks a significant milestone for the company as it transitions to public trading.

title: Palantir Technologies’ NYSE Debut
summary: Palantir Technologies, the Denver-based data-mining company co-founded by Peter Thiel, has gone public on the NYSE under the ticker PLTR. The company, valued at $20 billion after its last funding round, generated over 50% of its revenue from government contracts with agencies like the CIA and Department of Defense, sparking some controversy. Palantir forecasts around $1.05 billion in revenue for 2020.

title: Billionaire Peter Thiel: The man with the Golden Touch!
summary: This article highlights Peter Thiel's extensive success as an investor and entrepreneur, detailing his involvement in numerous successful company launches and sales. It covers his critical role in companies like PayPal and Palantir, and his current stakes in several significant ventures across diverse sectors, including those with recent significant IPOs and acquisitions. The piece also touches on his contrarian views and unique investment philosophy, often referred to as having the "Midas Touch."

title: Peter Thiel joins board of B.C. antibody therapy developer AbCellera in advance of expected IPO
summary: Billionaire venture capitalist Peter Thiel has joined the board of Vancouver-based AbCellera Biologics Inc. ahead of an anticipated Nasdaq IPO filing. Thiel is an existing investor in the company, which has gained global attention for its work in developing a COVID-19 treatment. AbCellera recently acquired Trianni Inc. for US$90-million and secured significant funding for its antibody discovery efforts.

title: Quid Secures $320M in Second Fund to Continue Liquidity and Option Exercise Loans for Private Company Employees
summary: Quid Capital Group has raised $320 million in a second fund to provide liquidity and option exercise loans for employees at high-growth private technology companies. This new fund, backed by Oaktree Capital Management, Davidson Kempner Capital Management LP, and a leading Ivy League endowment, will expand Quid's ability to offer capital to shareholders, allowing them to monetize their equity without selling their positions. Quid aims to provide solutions that optimize tax, option exercise, and liquidity planning for private company shareholders, helping companies retain talent and grow towards IPO.

title: Where Does Billionaire Investor Chamath Palihapitiya Invest His Money?
summary: Billionaire investor Chamath Palihapitiya, founder of Social Capital, primarily invests in tech startups and has extensively utilized Special Purpose Acquisition Companies (SPACs) to take companies public. His investment firm transitioned into a tech holding company, notably taking Virgin Galactic, Opendoor, MP Materials, and Clover Health public. Palihapitiya is also a strong proponent of Bitcoin, believing it could reach $1 million.

title: COVID Tech: Silicon Valley Exodus? Hewlett-Packard Enterprise Moving From San Jose To Houston
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is relocating its company headquarters from San Jose, California, to the Houston area in Texas, a move announced by Texas Gov. Greg Abbott. This relocation, set for early 2022, is part of a trend of tech companies expanding operations outside Silicon Valley, driven by factors like lower housing costs and talent recruitment opportunities in Texas. HPE emphasized that it will maintain a significant presence in Silicon Valley, particularly for innovation and talent, and that no layoffs are associated with the move.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise leaving Silicon Valley, moving to Texas
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) announced its decision to relocate its headquarters from San Jose, California, to Houston, Texas. This move, detailed in its fourth-quarter earnings report, positions Houston as the company's largest U.S. employment hub and a strategic location for talent acquisition, with a new campus under construction. The relocation marks another significant company departure from Silicon Valley, following similar moves by Dropbox and Palantir Technologies.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise becomes latest tech firm to leave Silicon Valley
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is relocating its headquarters from San Jose, California, to Houston, Texas, following a trend of tech companies moving out of Silicon Valley. This move, despite HPE's historical roots in Palo Alto, is driven by factors like California's high cost of living and the rise of remote work during the pandemic. The company also reported strong quarterly revenues that topped analyst predictions, suggesting businesses are upgrading data-center hardware.

title: Founding Father HPE Leaves Silicon Valley, Moves HQ To Texas
summary: Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (HPE), a company with roots as a founding member of Silicon Valley, announced its decision to relocate its headquarters from San Jose, California, to Houston, Texas. This move is attributed to Houston being an attractive market for talent and a broader re-evaluation influenced by high living costs in the Bay Area. While administrative functions will move to Texas, HPE's innovation hub will remain in San Jose, with no layoffs expected from this relocation.

title: What to Do About the Red-Hot Airbnb IPO Today
summary: Shah Gilani discusses the frenzy around recent IPOs like Snowflake (SNOW) and DoorDash (DASH), expressing concern about market froth reminiscent of the dot-com bubble. He analyzes Airbnb's (ABNB) valuation in light of the pandemic and advises a cautious approach for investors looking to buy ABNB shares, suggesting waiting for a lower price post-IPO.

title: Oracle is the latest Silicon Valley company to head for the hills — of Austin
summary: Oracle Corp. announced its decision to move its corporate headquarters from Redwood City, California, to Austin, Texas, following a trend of companies and executives relocating from Silicon Valley. This move aligns with a new flexible employee work location policy and positions Oracle for growth in a business-friendly state. Governor Greg Abbott lauded the relocation, emphasizing Texas's appeal for business, jobs, and opportunity, with other tech giants like Tesla and HP Enterprise also making similar moves or expanding in the state.

title: Oracle is moving its headquarters from Silicon Valley to Austin, Texas
summary: Oracle announced it is moving its headquarters from Redwood City, California, to Austin, Texas, becoming the latest tech company to relocate out of Silicon Valley. This move is part of a new flexible employee work location policy, allowing personnel more flexibility in where and how they work. Other tech companies like Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Palantir Technologies, as well as Elon Musk, have also moved or announced moves out of California.

title: Oracle moves headquarters to Texas from Silicon Valley
summary: Oracle Corp. announced it is relocating its corporate headquarters from Redwood City, California, to Austin, Texas. This move follows a trend of tech companies leaving Silicon Valley due to the increased feasibility of remote work and California's high operational costs and taxes. Oracle indicated that employees would have flexibility in choosing their work location, a decision aimed at positioning the company for growth.

title: Even More Tech Firms Are Leaving Silicon Valley for Texas
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is relocating its headquarters from Silicon Valley to Houston, Texas, following a trend of tech firms moving to states with more favorable business climates and lower costs. The article highlights that Texas offers significant financial advantages, such as no state income tax, lower housing costs, and a better business tax climate compared to California, attracting both companies and individuals like Elon Musk. This shift is prompting speculation that other major tech companies, such as Tesla, might follow suit, further solidifying Texas as an emerging tech hub.

title: Oracle moving its HQ from California to Texas, like HP Enterprise, Tesla CEO
summary: Oracle Corp. is moving its headquarters from Redwood City, California, to Austin, Texas, becoming the latest tech giant to leave Silicon Valley. The company cited a more flexible employee work location policy and positioning for growth as reasons for the move. This follows similar relocations by HP Enterprise and Tesla CEO Elon Musk to Texas, and Palantir Technologies to Denver, highlighting a trend of companies exiting California.
authors: Dallas Morning News and Dom Difurio

title: Why Oracle Made the Big Move From California to the Lone Star State
summary: Oracle relocated its headquarters from Redwood City, California, to Austin, Texas, in December 2020. The company cited a desire to provide its employees with more flexibility and benefit from Texas's business-friendly climate, including no personal or corporate income tax. This move follows a trend of other companies leaving California for Texas due to the latter's lower cost of living and doing business, skilled workforce, and robust infrastructure.

title: Why Bulls Should Consider Buying NNDM
summary: Nano Dimension Ltd. (NNDM) has seen its stock price soar by 277% in 2020, with significant gains in the last three months, despite missing revenue and EPS expectations. The company, which specializes in Additively Manufactured Electronics and 3-D printing machines, is considered to be in an early growth stage with a strong balance sheet, making it an attractive speculative play for investors.

title: Roblox Plans to Go Public Through Direct Listing
summary: Roblox Corp., a videogame company, announced its decision to go public through a direct listing after raising $520 million. This move comes a month after the company initially shelved its IPO plans. A recent funding round valued Roblox at $29.5 billion with shares priced at $45 apiece.

title: Merck Announces Formation of Merck Digital Ethics Advisory Panel
summary: Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany, has announced the formation of its Digital Ethics Advisory Panel to provide guidance on evolving digital business models and address ethical questions across its three business sectors. This new panel complements Merck's existing Bioethics Advisory Panel and will develop a Digital Code of Ethics to ensure responsible development of new digital technologies and ethical data usage. The panel will include academic and industry experts and will initially support Syntropy, a partnership focused on ethical healthcare data integration.

title: SAIC Awarded $95M DLA Contract Extension for Military Facility MRO Supplies
summary: Science Applications International Corp. (SAIC) has been awarded a one-year, $95 million contract extension from the Defense Logistics Agency (DLA). Under this bridge contract, SAIC will continue to provide maintenance, repair, and operation supplies for U.S. military facilities. The contract will support various branches of the U.S. military, with work performed in multiple states and Washington D.C.

title: Satellite Data Service Market SWOT Analysis by Key Players: Trimble, Planet Labs, Imagesat International
summary: HTF Market Intelligence has released a report on the global Satellite Data Service Market, offering a detailed analysis of market size, competitive landscape, and future growth prospects up to 2026. title: As Change Comes to Washington, One Analyst Is Nervous About Palantir
summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) shares are declining due to ongoing concerns about its valuation and potential challenges with the U.S. government, its primary customer, following the change in the White House. An analyst has expressed nervousness about the big-data analytics company's immediate future.

title: Constellation’s Holger Mueller on Salesforce, Oracle, AWS and the future of cloud software
summary: Holger Mueller, vice president and principal analyst at Constellation Research Inc., discusses major trends in the cloud industry, including Salesforce's evolution into a platform company with the Slack acquisition. He also touches on Oracle's strong competitive position with its database focus and AWS's strategy in bridging cloud with on-premises workloads. Mueller foresees SaaS models continuing to drive cost reduction and highlights the increasing importance of AI and machine learning in the future of cloud software.

title: Why some tech companies and billionaires are leaving California
summary: Some major tech companies and wealthy individuals are relocating their headquarters and residences out of California to states like Texas, Arizona, and Florida. This exodus is attributed to high taxes, the cost of living, and heavy regulations, exacerbated by the rise of remote work during the pandemic. Companies like Oracle, Palantir, and Hewlett-Packard Enterprise have moved, while others, including Dropbox, Twitter, and Facebook, have embraced permanent remote work, leading many Bay Area workers to consider leaving the region.

title: Palantir Technologies and Rio Tinto Sign Multi-Year Enterprise Partnership
summary: Palantir Technologies has signed a multi-year enterprise agreement with Rio Tinto for its Foundry Platform. The software will integrate data from various sources to provide a single source of truth for critical mining operations, aiming to improve safety, cost, and production. This partnership expands on previous successful data integration projects between the two companies.

title: How the LAPD and Palantir Use Data to Justify Racist Policing
summary: A new book by sociologist Sarah Brayne, "Predict and Surveil: Data, Discretion, and the Future of Policing," examines how the LAPD and other law enforcement agencies use data-driven policing technologies, including software from Palantir and PredPol, to justify biased practices. Brayne's research, based on months embedded with the LAPD, reveals how these technologies, often marketed as objective solutions, instead amplify existing biases and expand surveillance without necessarily improving public safety. The article highlights how systems like Operation LASER and automated license plate readers create "secondary surveillance networks," catching innocent individuals in a cycle of increased scrutiny, despite skepticism from some officers about these technologies' actual effectiveness.

title: Palantir Stock Upgraded to 'Buy' With a Fresh $34 Price Target From Goldman Sachs
summary: Goldman Sachs upgraded Palantir Technologies to a "buy" rating with a $34 price target, citing strong Q4 results that surpassed revenue and EBITDA expectations. Analysts highlighted the company's robust government revenue, significant margin expansion, and growing backlog as indicators of sustainable long-term growth. This positive outlook led to a substantial increase in Goldman's price target and prompted Cathie Wood's ARK Next Generation Internet ETF to purchase 1.56 million shares.

title: Akin Gump and Palantir Collaborate on Legal Digital Service Platform
summary: Akin Gump Strauss Hauer & Feld LLP and Palantir Technologies Inc. announced their collaboration to introduce Akin Gump’s RegSpot, a new legal digital services platform powered by Palantir’s Foundry software. This platform aims to provide innovative solutions for clients' legal needs, specifically by streamlining complex compliance processes. The first module, the "MEU Tool," will assist clients with due diligence for U.S. government trade restrictions involving "military end use/users."

title: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest ETFs acquired over 6.8 million shares of Palantir in the last week as the stock dipped
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest ETFs, specifically ARKK and ARKW, significantly increased their holdings in Palantir, acquiring over 6.8 million shares worth about $172 million after a dip in the stock price. This move followed Palantir's surprise earnings loss and a lockup expiration that led to a six-day losing streak for the company. Despite some analyst bearishness due to its valuation and reliance on government revenue, Wood remains confident in Palantir's long-term growth prospects, echoing enthusiasm shown by some Goldman Sachs analysts and Reddit traders.

title: Palantir sinks 13% as filings show 3 top executives offloaded 2.7 million shares following lockup expiration
summary: Palantir's stock dropped 13% after regulatory filings revealed that co-founder Stephen Cohen and two other top executives sold 2.7 million shares following the company's lockup expiration. Despite this insider selling, which has been a trend since its IPO, Goldman Sachs recently gave Palantir a "buy" rating, citing sustainable growth potential, and Cathie Wood's ARK Invest has been adding shares. The insider sales occurred at prices ranging from $25-$30, coming after the stock had seen significant increases post-IPO.

title: Cramer's lightning round: United Microelectronics is a good idea
summary: Jim Cramer conducted his "lightning round," offering quick takes on various stocks. He endorsed United Microelectronics due to the high demand and shortage of integrated circuits. Cramer also discussed Enterprise Products Partners, GlaxoSmithKline, and Palantir.

title: Can Starbucks Stock Remain Unfazed by the Broad-Market Selloff?
summary: Starbucks (SBUX) stock has shown resilience despite a broader market downturn, with its 80-day moving average containing recent losses. The company is set to hold its annual shareholder meeting, which could lead to analyst upgrades and further boost the stock. While SBUX has a high current price-earnings ratio, its forward P/E is significantly lower, and its options currently suggest low volatility expectations, making it a potential entry point for long-term investors.

title: Cathie Wood's Ark Innovation ETF surges more than 10% for its best day ever
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK) surged over 10% for its best day ever, driven by a rebound in tech stocks like Tesla, Zoom Video, and Palantir. This rally helped recoup some recent sharp losses for the fund, which had been impacted by a market rotation out of high-growth tech into value stocks. Despite recent volatility, Wood remains confident in her disruptive strategy, viewing the sell-off as an opportunity to buy.

title: Upstart shares are up 171% in three days, as momentum pours into fintech company
summary: Upstart's shares surged 171% in three days, closing at $164.87, pushing its market cap past $12 billion after going public in December. This momentum follows strong fourth-quarter revenue growth of 39% and an optimistic first-quarter forecast, with the company benefiting from increased demand for its AI-driven loan underwriting technology. Despite the rapid rise reminiscent of meme stocks, analysts express caution about its high valuation, even as they raise price targets.

title: Don’t Day Trade PLTR Stock. Buy and Hold It for the Long Term
summary: The article advises against day trading Palantir (PLTR) stock and instead recommends a long-term buy-and-hold strategy due to its high-growth potential. It discusses Palantir's strong revenue growth, increasing partnerships with government and commercial entities, and its rich valuation, suggesting that despite potential volatility, it could be a core component of a growth portfolio. Investors are encouraged to consider buying dips for the long haul or investing via ETFs that hold PLTR.

title: CEO Stuart Bradie on KBR’s Space, Directed Energy Work
summary: KBR President and CEO Stuart Bradie discussed the company's focus on space, defense, and intelligence in an interview with CNBC. He highlighted KBR's significant contributions to NASA, including supporting the International Space Station, and its leadership in directed energy technology for defense against drone swarms. Bradie also mentioned KBR's restructuring to streamline its business into government and technology solutions, emphasizing a shift towards high-value, technology-driven markets.

title: BP Hits Net Debt Target Ahead of Plan, with Shareholders Likely to Benefit, Says Looney
summary: BP has reached its $35 billion net debt target in the first quarter of 2021, ahead of schedule, due to early disposal proceeds and strong business performance. CEO Bernard Looney announced that the company plans to return at least 60% of surplus cash flow to shareholders via share buybacks, provided it maintains a strong investment grade credit rating. This achievement follows BP's strategic shift to become an "integrated energy producer" and net-zero company by 2050.

title: Cathie Wood's ARK Space ETF is the largest shareholder of Cathie Wood's 3D printing ETF
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX) has become the largest shareholder of her own 3D printing ETF (PRNT), holding nearly 5.8% of the fund. ARKX, which launched recently, holds significantly more shares than other major investors, indicating Wood is buying into her own 3D printing fund amidst a recent dip in its performance. This move aligns with Wood's continued confidence in her high-flying tech stock picks despite market rotations.

title: Coinbase goes public with a nearly $100 billion valuation
summary: Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase Global went public with a valuation of nearly $100 billion, with shares opening at $381 and closing at $328. The direct listing capitalized on the surging demand for digital currencies and market legitimacy, turning CEO Brian Armstrong into a mega-billionaire. The event is seen as a significant milestone for the crypto industry, attracting further institutional attention and investment.

title: Sunnyvale real estate mega-deal makes Google, big developer neighbors
summary: Tishman Speyer completed a major real estate transaction in Sunnyvale, acquiring seven parcels from NetApp for $365 million, making them neighbors with Google. This deal includes four large office buildings and amenities, giving Tishman Speyer significant control in a prime Silicon Valley tech area. Google also owns adjacent properties, suggesting potential long-term redevelopment plans for both entities in northern Sunnyvale.

title: Palantir and Ringier Extend Digital Transformation Partnership
summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. and Ringier AG have renewed their strategic partnership, allowing Ringier to continue using Palantir's Foundry software for digital transformation. This collaboration helps Ringier enhance its data utilization to better serve readers, leading to increased revenue for quality journalism and improved advertising performance. The Foundry platform integrates siloed data, enabling data-driven decision-making and supporting Ringier's transition to a digital-first global media company.

title: SAIC to Acquire Data Management Tech Developer Koverse; Nazzic Keene Quoted
summary: Science Applications International Corp. (SAIC) has announced an agreement to acquire Koverse, a software company specializing in data management technology, for an undisclosed sum. This acquisition aims to bolster SAIC's analytics and artificial intelligence capabilities, leveraging Koverse's platform and its established presence with government and commercial clients. Nazzic Keene, SAIC's CEO, highlighted the strategic importance of Koverse's technology in enhancing SAIC's data modernization offerings across federal defense, civilian, and intelligence sectors.

title: Roivant’s move to go public reveals just how much its drug strategy has changed
summary: Roivant Sciences is going public through a merger with SPAC Montes Archimedes Acquisition Corp., revealing a significant shift in its strategy from acquiring overlooked drugs to investing heavily in computational drug discovery and development technologies. The deal values the new company at an expected market capitalization of $7.3 billion and provides $611 million in new capital to support its transformed focus. Roivant's evolution includes forming tech subsidiaries like Lokavant and Datavant, and making strategic acquisitions like Silicon Therapeutics, emphasizing AI and machine learning in its drug pipeline.

title: Earnings Previews: Palantir, Roblox, 3D Systems, Tilray, Velodyne
summary: This article provides an earnings preview for five companies: Roblox, 3D Systems, Tilray, Velodyne Lidar, and Palantir. It details analyst expectations for their upcoming quarterly reports, including EPS, revenue forecasts, and stock performance since their respective IPOs or recent periods. The piece highlights investor sentiment, analyst ratings, and price targets for each company.

title: Executive Mosaic CEO Jim Garrettson Presents Fifth Wash100 Award to DeEtte Gray, President of CACI Business & IT Solutions
summary: Executive Mosaic CEO Jim Garrettson presented the 2021 Wash100 Award to DeEtte Gray, President of CACI's Business and IT Solutions, marking her fifth recognition. Gray was honored for her leadership and contributions to the government contracting sector, particularly for advancing CACI's IT capabilities and securing significant contract awards in 2020. This includes a notable $1.5 billion contract with the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency to enhance its cybersecurity.

title: Oracle Boasted That Its Software Was Used Against U.S. Protesters. Then It Took the Tech to China.
summary: Oracle promoted its Endeca software, initially developed with CIA backing and used by the Chicago Police Department to monitor U.S. protesters during the 2012 NATO summit, to Chinese authorities for surveillance purposes. This included marketing the software for "predictive policing" and social media mining in China, a move that critics argue contradicts U.S. intelligence interests and civil liberties protections. The article highlights concerns about big tech companies like Oracle entering the policing space and selling invasive surveillance tools globally after testing them domestically.

title: ZipRecruiter goes public: What to know about ZIP’s direct listing on the NYSE
summary: ZipRecruiter is going public today through a direct listing on the NYSE, following in the footsteps of companies like Coinbase and Slack. This method allows current shareholders, rather than the company itself, to sell shares. The job marketplace, founded in 2010, has a reference price of $18 per share, valuing the company at approximately $2.4 billion based on its 133.1 million shares.

title: People Moves: Island Insurance Names Kuioka COO, The Liberty Company Insurance Brokers Adds Newman in California
summary: Island Insurance Co. has promoted Scott Kuioka to senior vice president and chief operating officer. Kuioka will also retain his roles as chief investment officer and chief risk officer, overseeing several key departments. Additionally, The Liberty Company Insurance Brokers appointed Lenny Newman as a vice president in their San Francisco Bay Area office, where he will specialize in trucking and lawyer’s professional liability.

title: L3Harris to Support Army Depot’s Repair Work on USAF’s MQ-9 Drone Control System
summary: L3Harris Technologies will collaborate with Tobyhanna Army Depot to repair components of the ground control station for the U.S. Air Force's MQ-9 Reaper drone. This partnership aims to sustain the depot's competitiveness through industry engagement and builds upon years of collaboration between the two entities since 2016. The work focuses on repairing line repairable units of the GCS, which is essential for remotely controlling the MQ-9 Reaper's intelligence-gathering missions.

title: Snowflake becomes latest tech company to leave Silicon Valley
summary: Cloud software company Snowflake has joined a growing list of tech firms departing Silicon Valley, announcing it no longer has a corporate headquarters and instead operates with a globally distributed workforce. While still maintaining a large office in Silicon Valley, the company designated its Bozeman, Montana office as its principal executive office due to its CEO and CFO being based there. This move reflects a broader trend among major tech companies to leave Silicon Valley, citing high costs, taxes, and perceived anti-business sentiment.

title: Palantir and DoorDash CEOs Top List of Biggest Pay Packages in 2020
summary: Alexander Karp of Palantir Technologies and Tony Xu of DoorDash received the largest compensation packages in 2020, with Karp's valued at $1.1 billion and Xu's at over $400 million, largely due to special stock awards before their companies went public. These packages significantly surpassed the median pay for S&P 500 CEOs, which was $13.4 million, and even the biggest S&P 500 compensation package of $211 million that year.

title: Does Palantir’s share price performance match its CEO’s salary?
summary: This article examines whether Palantir CEO Alex Karp's $1.1 billion 2020 compensation is justified by the company's share price performance, especially given its lack of profitability. It details Karp's compensation breakdown, Palantir's stock performance since its direct listing, and analyst opinions on its valuation and growth prospects, highlighting its reliance on government contracts and popularity among retail investors.

title: Bernstein recommends Sea, says Singapore-based internet company is a 'super app in the making'
summary: Bernstein recommends Sea Ltd., calling the Singapore-based internet company a "super app in the making" and initiating coverage with an outperform rating and a $350 price target. The investment firm believes Sea's strong execution track record ensures its dominance in various verticals, particularly highlighting the potential of its e-commerce arm, Shopee, to reach breakeven by 2025. Key drivers for the stock are expected to be Shopee's expansion in Latin America and grocery in Southeast Asia, leading to increased profitability.

title: Babylon Health Stock: Value-Based Care Using Telehealth
summary: Babylon Health, a London-based company known for its AI healthcare chatbot, is merging with a SPAC (Alkuri Global Acquisition Corp. KURI) to expand its telehealth business, particularly in the U.S. market. The article discusses Babylon's history, its AI technology which claims to out-diagnose doctors in some cases, and its shift towards a "value-based care" model where it takes responsibility for a patient's health from cradle to grave. Despite rapid expansion and projected revenue growth, the author expresses caution due to the complexities of the U.S. healthcare system and the inherent risks with SPAC mergers, suggesting Teladoc (TDOC) remains a better investment in the telehealth space.

title: Lord of the Roths: How Tech Mogul Peter Thiel Turned a Retirement Account for the Middle Class Into a $5 Billion Tax-Free Piggy Bank
summary: This article exposes how tech mogul Peter Thiel transformed a Roth IRA, intended for middle-class retirement savings, into a tax-free $5 billion fortune. Using confidential IRS data, ProPublica reveals that Thiel and other ultrawealthy individuals exploit loopholes, such as buying startup shares at extremely low valuations, to amass enormous untaxed wealth in these accounts. The report highlights how Congress and the IRS have failed to curb these abuses, leading to massive tax breaks for the rich while most Americans struggle with retirement savings.

title: Billionaire investor Peter Thiel has $5B in his tax-free retirement account, report finds
summary: A ProPublica report alleges that billionaire investor Peter Thiel grew his Roth IRA to $5 billion, largely tax-free, by investing early shares of PayPal and Facebook. This practice, while legal, raises questions about the effectiveness and fairness of the current tax system, according to tax experts and lawmakers. The report highlights how the wealthy can use retirement accounts, intended for middle-class savings, to accumulate vast, tax-exempt fortunes from capital gains.

title: Palantir, DataRobot Partner to Bring Speed and Agility to Demand Forecasting Models
summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. and DataRobot have partnered to create a new framework aimed at revolutionizing demand forecasting for retailers. This collaboration combines Palantir Foundry and DataRobot's model development capabilities to enable businesses to create and test data-driven forecasting models rapidly. The partnership seeks to address challenges posed by outdated models and increasing data complexity, ultimately improving forecast accuracy and optimizing supply chain management.

title: Origin Materials, Market Leader in Disruptive Materials Technology, Completes Business Combination With Artius, Creating First Publicly Traded Pure Play Carbon Negative Materials Company
summary: Origin Materials has completed its business combination with Artius Acquisition Inc., forming the first publicly traded pure-play carbon-negative materials company. The combined entity will trade on Nasdaq under the symbol "ORGN" starting June 25, 2021. The transaction, valued at approximately $1 billion, is supported by existing and new investors and will fund the company's growth and commercial plant operations.

title: Wejo Partners with Microsoft, Palantir Technologies and Sompo Holdings to Transform the Future of Connected Vehicle Data
summary: Wejo, a global leader in connected vehicle data, has partnered with Microsoft, Palantir Technologies, and Sompo Holdings to advance connected vehicle data applications worldwide. These partnerships aim to establish a manufacturer-agnostic industry standard for connected vehicle data, creating applications across various sectors and leveraging Microsoft Azure for data and intelligence solutions. Furthermore, Microsoft and Sompo have committed an additional $25 million investment as part of Wejo's $125 million PIPE financing, signaling strong support for Wejo's vision and public listing plans.

title: Blend Labs Hits A Nearly $4 Billion Valuation As It Makes Its NYSE Debut
summary: Blend Labs, a San Francisco-based mortgage software company, debuted on the NYSE on Friday with its shares hitting $18, valuing the company at nearly $4 billion. The IPO raised $360 million, and the company's cloud-based platform is used by 31 of the top 100 U.S. financial firms, processing $5 billion worth of loans daily. Cofounder Nima Ghamsari, who stands to gain a significant payout if the company's stock performs well, expects finance to be truly digital and real-time in 10 years, with Blend becoming one of the biggest companies globally.

title: High volatility in Verb Technology Company Inc. stock price on Friday which ended trading at $2.92
summary: Verb Technology Company Inc. (VERB) stock experienced high volatility, falling -8.75% on July 16, 2021, to $2.92, despite a 52.88% rise over the preceding two weeks. The stock shows short and long-term buy signals from moving averages and a strong rising trend, leading to a "Buy" recommendation from StockInvest.us. However, a sell signal was issued from a recent pivot top, and the stock is considered "very high risk" due to its volatility and wide prediction interval.

title: Palantir Introduces Foundry for Builders
summary: The provided content is a navigation and footer section from Palantir's Investor Relations page, not an article about the introduction of "Foundry for Builders." It lists various investor resources like quarterly results, SEC filings, governance documents, and contact information. There is no news article content to summarize.

title: Palantir Introduces Foundry for Builders
summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. announced the launch of Foundry for Builders, an initiative providing its Foundry platform to early-stage companies to support their growth. This expands Palantir's business beyond large organizations to younger companies across various sectors, enabling them to build data-driven operations from day one. The program offers Foundry as a fully managed SaaS and has already onboarded several start-ups, including those in healthcare, robotics, and fintech.

title: Palette Token、申込金額が224億を突破
summary: Coincheck's IEO for Palette Token saw an application amount exceeding 22.4 billion yen, with an application倍率 of 24.11 times. On its first day of trading, the token reached a high of approximately 11.5 times its selling price. Coincheck IEO aims to support token ecosystem formation and fundraising for companies and projects.

title: Kredivo, the Leading Digital Consumer Credit Platform in Southeast Asia, Announces Plans to Become a Publicly Traded Company via Merger with VPC Impact Acquisition Holdings II
summary: Kredivo, a leading digital consumer credit platform in Southeast Asia, and VPC Impact Acquisition Holdings II (VPCB), a SPAC sponsored by Victory Park Capital, have announced a definitive agreement for a business combination. This merger will result in FinAccel, Kredivo’s parent company, becoming publicly traded with an expected pro forma equity value of approximately $2.5 billion. The transaction is anticipated to deliver over $430 million in gross proceeds to the company, enabling Kredivo's continued growth in Indonesia, expansion into regional markets like Vietnam and Thailand, and entry into new business lines.

title: BlackSky and Palantir Collaboration Aids Government Agencies in Predicting Events
summary: BlackSky and Palantir have completed a joint pilot program that integrates BlackSky's satellite imagery and analytics into Palantir Foundry, demonstrating the ability to provide real-time actionable intelligence for defense and intelligence customers. This collaboration aims to deliver worldwide intelligence rapidly, aiding in predicting events for time-sensitive operations. The companies highlight the significant impact of combining global monitoring from space with deep analytics to inform strategic decision-making.

title: Warning Sign: Palantir Buys $50M in Gold Bars
summary: Palantir Technologies Inc., a company with Peter Thiel as its largest shareholder, made headlines by purchasing $50.7 million worth of 100-ounce gold bars. This investment in gold stands out as many other U.S. companies are currently focusing on cryptocurrencies. The gold will initially be stored by a third party in the Northeastern United States, with Palantir retaining the ability to take physical delivery.

title: Gold, an "Attractive Insurance Policy"
summary: Frank Holmes argues that gold serves as an attractive insurance policy for U.S. taxpayers and investors against government spending and currency debasement, much like why poppy cultivation is an "attractive insurance policy" for the Taliban. He highlights gold's lack of counterparty risk and notes that Palantir Technologies recently invested $50 million in gold bars. Despite current gold price suppression, potentially due to market manipulation, Holmes suggests that gold miners and royalty/streaming companies could be a buy, anticipating a rebound in gold prices.

title: Exclusive | FBI Palantir glitch allowed unauthorized access to private data
summary: A glitch in the Palantir software used by the FBI allowed unauthorized employees to access private data for over a year, specifically data from the case against accused hacker Virgil Griffith. While prosecutors stated Palantir's default settings enabled this access, Palantir maintains there was no software glitch and the issue stemmed from the FBI not following proper protocols. The incident raises concerns about the broader implications for data privacy and FBI data handling practices.

title: FBI blooper allowed agents to use Palantir to see restricted material -letter
summary: An FBI error allowed agents to improperly access restricted evidence using Palantir Technologies software, despite the data being intended for segregation. According to a letter from a U.S. prosecutor, the data was mistakenly tagged as unrestricted, enabling four FBI employees unconnected to the prosecution to view it for over 15 months. Palantir stated its platform had strong security controls and that the error stemmed from the end-user not following FBI protocols.

title: BlackSky Secures Investment from Palantir and Enters into Multi-Year Strategic Partnership Following Successful Pilot Project
summary: BlackSky announced an equity investment from Palantir Technologies Inc. and a multi-year strategic partnership following a successful joint pilot project. This collaboration aims to expand the delivery of high-resolution imagery and deep analytics to customers worldwide, integrating BlackSky's capabilities with Palantir Foundry. The partnership is set to accelerate BlackSky's go-to-market plan and meet increasing demand for global monitoring services.

title: Keycorp Stock Shows Rising Relative Strength Amid Surging Profit Growth
summary: Keycorp (KEY) saw its Relative Strength (RS) Rating increase to 77 due to strong earnings reports, including a 205% and 350% surge in year-over-year profit growth. Despite a fractional dip in its stock price reflecting market consolidation, the upgrade highlights the super-regional bank's positive momentum.

title: Confluent Stock, MongoDB, Sprout Social Among 9 New Stocks On IBD Watchlists
summary: Investor's Business Daily (IBD) has updated its stock watchlists, adding new companies like Confluent, MongoDB, and Sprout Social, while removing others that show weakness. These lists, including the IBD 50, Sector Leaders, and IBD Big Cap 20, are regularly revised to reflect current market conditions and identify potential investment opportunities. The changes help investors stay informed about leading stocks and those nearing buy points.
authors: INVESTOR'S BUSINESS DAILY and MARK SHARAR

title: Faraday Future Announces Partnership With Palantir to Develop Disruptive Products & Services
summary: Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. announced a partnership with Palantir Foundry at its annual ‘919 Futurist Day’. Palantir has made a strategic investment in FF, enabling the development of disruptive products and services and joint exploration of innovations across the mobility value chain. This collaboration aims to accelerate capabilities like autonomous driving and redefine aftersales and financial services, with FF confident in delivering its FF 91 Futurist within 12 months of closing.

title: Hatten Land Brings ‘Green’ Crypto Mining Activities to Melaka With SMI Partnership
summary: Hatten Land Limited has announced a partnership with Singapore Myanmar Investco Ltd. (SMI) to establish "green" cryptocurrency mining operations in Melaka, Malaysia. This initiative involves using solar panels on Hatten Land's retail malls to power the mining activities, aligning with the company's pivot towards a more environmentally friendly digital economy. Additionally, Hatten Land is undertaking a share placement to raise S$1.8 million to fund its technology ventures and renewable energy initiatives.

title: Another Electric Vehicle ETF to Keep an Eye On
summary: The article highlights the Global X Lithium & Battery Tech ETF (LIT) as an attractive investment in the electric vehicle industry, noting its 33% year-to-date growth and $5 billion in assets. Senior Market Strategist Matthew Timpane discusses LIT's strong performance, its significant holdings in Elba Merrill and BYD, and key technical support levels, particularly around the $75 mark. He suggests that LIT represents a long-term exposure opportunity within the booming EV sector.

title: Quantum computer maker Rigetti to go public via $1.5 bln SPAC deal
summary: Quantum computer maker Rigetti & Co Inc announced its plan to go public through a merger with a blank-check firm, Supernova Partners Acquisition Company II Ltd, in a deal valued potentially at $1.5 billion. This move makes Rigetti the second quantum computer hardware maker to list publicly this year via a SPAC. The merger is expected to provide Rigetti with approximately $458 million in proceeds, including significant investments from major financial firms and strategic partners like Palantir Technologies Inc.

title: Quantum computing startup Rigetti plans to go public through SPAC merger
summary: Rigetti Computing Inc., a prominent quantum computing startup, announced its plan to go public by merging with a special-purpose acquisition company, Supernova Partners Acquisition Company II Ltd. The deal values Rigetti at approximately $1.5 billion and is expected to provide $458 million in cash. Rigetti, which will trade on the NYSE under "RGTI," has unveiled a new modular architecture and an updated roadmap, aiming to build a 1,000-qubit system by 2024 and a 4,000-qubit system by 2026.

title: Best Stock Options to Buy Today – Here They Are
summary: The article discusses how to identify cheap stock options by focusing on implied volatility (IV) being at the low end of its 52-week range. It highlights United Natural Foods Inc. (UNFI) as an example, explaining how "volatility crush" after earnings reports can create opportunities to buy options at a discount. The author, Tom Gentile, suggests that the post-earnings period is ideal for trading options.

The article discusses the growing trend of enormous special stock awards for startup founders, often far exceeding compensation for CEOs of established companies. It highlights examples like Archer Aviation, Palantir Technologies, and GoodRx Holdings, whose founders received nine-figure packages even before their companies were profitable or generated revenue. This practice contrasts with earlier Silicon Valley norms where founders like Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos relied on the appreciation of their initial equity, and it raises concerns about shareholder dilution and corporate governance.

title: Men Taking 6 Months Parental Leave Are ‘Losers,’ Top VC Says
summary: Joe Lonsdale, a prominent venture capitalist and Palantir co-founder, stirred controversy by calling men who take six months of parental leave "losers" in response to criticism directed at Pete Buttigieg. This sparked a strong backlash, with other venture capitalists like Alexis Ohanian and Garry Tan defending their four-month parental leave policies. Lonsdale later walked back the use of the word "loser" but reiterated his stance on traditional gender roles.

title: Continued Strong Third Quarter U.S. IPO Activity Pushes 2021 to New Highs
summary: U.S. IPO activity in the third quarter of 2021 remained high, with 175 companies going public, bringing the year-to-date total to 785—a new record. While overall volume held steady, gross proceeds for the quarter declined, and the average IPO size decreased. SPAC IPOs rebounded slightly but stayed below earlier peaks, with the Finance sector continuing to lead in volume driven by SPACs.

title: Palantir and AlixPartners Join Forces to Help Clients Unlock the Value of Data
summary: AlixPartners and Palantir Technologies have formed a strategic partnership to help clients leverage data for improved business outcomes. This collaboration will combine AlixPartners' consulting services with Palantir's Foundry software platform to address complex challenges across various industries. The focus will be on driving data-driven decision-making, operational transformation, and developing new business models and customer interactions.

title: American Industrial Icon To Emerge As Aviation Pure-Play After 3-Way Split
summary: General Electric (GE) announced plans to split into three separate public companies, focusing on healthcare, power/renewable energy, and aviation. This restructuring aims to streamline the American industrial conglomerate after a period of significant change. Following the split, GE will emerge as an aviation pure-play, with its healthcare business spinning off in 2023 and the power and renewable energy businesses combining and separating in early 2024.

title: Largest U.S. Pension Bought Palantir, Snowflake, and Berkshire Hathaway Stock.
summary: The largest U.S. public pension, Calpers, made significant changes to its portfolio in the third quarter. It initiated stakes in analytics companies Palantir Technologies and Snowflake, and increased its holdings in Berkshire Hathaway. Conversely, Calpers fully divested its position in JD.com.

title: Stanley Druckenmiller is Selling These 5 Stocks
summary: Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller is reducing his stakes in several companies, including a significant decrease in his holdings of Intuit Inc. During the third quarter of 2021, Druckenmiller's hedge fund decreased its shares in Intuit by 51%, though it still held over $47 million worth of the company's stock. Intuit recently reported strong first fiscal quarter earnings, beating estimates with $1.53 EPS and a 52% year-over-year revenue increase to $2 billion.

title: BlackSky Achieves World’s Highest Revisit, Time-Diverse Dawn-to-Dusk Satellite Constellation with Three Successful Launches in Three Weeks
summary: BlackSky has achieved the world's highest revisit satellite constellation with a peak rate of 15 hourly visits per day, following the successful launch of six satellites across three missions in less than 30 days. This expansion brings their total constellation to 12 small satellites, significantly increasing imaging capacity and enabling their Spectra AI platform to deliver real-time geospatial intelligence. CEO Brian E. O’Toole highlights the company's rapid deployment capability and readiness to scale services for strong commercial and government market demand.

title: Wall Street shrugs at newly public stocks' woes ahead of 2022
summary: A Fortune analysis reveals that about half of the 100 largest U.S. IPOs in 2021 are trading below their offering price, indicating a recent souring of investor sentiment towards newly public stocks despite a blockbuster year for listings. Factors like spiking inflation, the Omicron variant, and a more hawkish Federal Reserve stance are contributing to investors rethinking riskier assets. Despite these challenges, experts anticipate a strong IPO market in 2022, especially for private equity-backed companies and those in fast-growing sectors.

title: Investments & Financials Archives
summary: This article provides an archive of Stagwell's investment and financial news, detailing several press releases from late 2025 and early 2026. Key announcements include the reporting of 2025 financial results, the launch of Quarter Creek Ventures for incubating new marketing technology, advancements in privacy-protective AI and data platforms through partnerships with Palantir and Harvard's OpenDP, and strategic investments in news media like Real Clear Holdings. It also covers various investor conference participations and outlines Stagwell's financial performance with positive growth in revenue and adjusted EBITDA for Q3 2025.

title: A Look Back and Ahead at Meme Stocks BlackBerry and Palantir
summary: This article examines the meme stock status of BlackBerry (BB) and Palantir (PLTR), comparing their stock performance and company fundamentals. It highlights BlackBerry's January 2021 surge due to meme stock trading and Palantir's November 2020 rise linked to its strong earnings and AI story, distinguishing their short interest profiles. The piece also provides financial snapshots and analyst ratings for both companies, noting that analysts are more optimistic about Palantir than BlackBerry.

title: Executive Spotlight With SAIC SVP Vincent DiFronzo Discusses Company's Work on WESTAR Program With Air Force
summary: In an Executive Spotlight interview, Vincent DiFronzo, SVP for Air Force and COCOM business at SAIC, discussed his transition from military to defense contracting. He highlighted SAIC's work on the Weapon Engagement Simulation Technology for Advanced Research (WESTAR) program for the U.S. Air Force. DiFronzo emphasized the company's role in modeling modern weapon systems, including hypersonic systems, for both the Air Force and the Missile Defense Agency.

title: Federal Tech Vet Ron Bewtra Named HPE High-Performance Computing Director
summary: Ron Bewtra, an experienced federal information technology veteran, has joined Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) as director of high-performance computing. Bewtra previously served as CTO at the Department of Justice and co-chair of the U.S. Federal Chief Information Officers Council’s innovation committee, bringing extensive public and private sector experience to his new role. He will manage offerings within HPE's leadership computing vertical segment.

title: Palantir Introduces Certification Program to Scale Platform and Empower Customers and Strategic Partners
summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. (NYSE:PLTR) has launched a new Certification Program to help customers accelerate value creation and scale by optimizing Foundry talent. The program offers certifications across four tracks: Data Engineer, Application Developer, Data Science, and Data Analyst, aiming to empower users and partners to utilize Palantir's software for organizational transformation. This initiative is part of Palantir's ongoing effort to support customers in unlocking value from their software and expanding Foundry expertise.

title: ALSP Orchid Acquisition Co. I (ALOR) Stock Price, News & Analysis $ALOR
summary: This article provides detailed stock information for ALSP Orchid Acquisition Co. I (ALOR). The company is noted as potentially delisted.

title: Palantir Technologies Extends Partnership with Ferrari to Bring Data Driven Performance Decisions to Race Operations
summary: Palantir Technologies has announced the extension of its partnership with Ferrari. This collaboration aims to integrate data-driven performance decisions into Ferrari's race operations, leveraging Palantir's expertise in data analytics.

title: Antelope Acquires Digital Pet Insurance Provider Doggo
summary: Antelope, a pet wellness platform, has acquired Doggo Insurance, a digital pet insurance provider available in all 50 states and partnered with Crum & Forster Pet Insurance Group. Doggo's plans cover various pet health expenses, including alternative therapy, end-of-life costs, and prescription pet food, and are underwritten by United States Fire Insurance Company. Antelope was founded in 2021 by Wendy Wen in collaboration with Alpine Investors.

title: Palantir Technologies Extends Partnership with Ferrari to Bring Data Driven Performance Decisions to Race Operations
summary: Palantir Technologies (NYSE:PLTR) has extended its partnership with Ferrari for another year, making it an official Team Partner of Scuderia Ferrari. The expanded agreement will see Scuderia Ferrari's Power Unit department continue to use Palantir's Foundry platform for data-driven performance decisions, integrating and analyzing large volumes of information from various sources to optimize race car performance. This collaboration highlights the crucial role of data analysis in Formula 1, enabling faster and more critical decisions for the team.

title: Palantir Technologies (PLTR): Partnership With Ferrari (RACE) Extended
summary: Palantir Technologies has extended its partnership with Ferrari for another year, integrating its data and analytics technology with Scuderia Ferrari's Power Unit department. This collaboration will utilize Palantir's Foundry platform to optimize race car performance by rapidly analyzing vast amounts of data from various sources, helping the team make crucial decisions during races. The partnership also involves Palantir's brand appearing on Ferrari's Formula 1 cars and driver suits.

title: Jacobs and Palantir Launch Global Strategic Partnership for Data Solutions
summary: Jacobs and Palantir Technologies Inc. have announced a global strategic partnership to deliver data and technology solutions, initially focusing on the water sector. This collaboration aims to optimize water and wastewater treatment plant operations, reduce costs, and improve performance through joint data analytics and Palantir's Foundry operating system. Both companies expect to expand these solutions to address broader data and cyber challenges in infrastructure and national security.

title: Alphabet’s (GOOGL) Stock Price Has Fallen 12.8% in 2022. Should You Buy It?
summary: Alphabet's stock (GOOGL) has dropped 12.8% in 2022, leading many to question if it's a good buying opportunity. Despite plateauing ad revenues, Google is expanding into cloud services and cybersecurity, highlighted by its acquisition of Mandiant. The company is considered attractive at current prices, though antitrust concerns remain a risk.

title: Videoconferencing Blockbuster: HP Inc. To Acquire Poly For $3.3B

(GRRR) saw its stock price surge by 34.1% following its strong first-quarter 2025 earnings report, driven by significant contract wins, global expansion, and sound financial management. The company issued optimistic guidance for 2025, anticipating revenues between $100-$110 million and a net profit of $15-$20 million, supported by a substantial project pipeline. With its involvement in the "ONE AMAZON" project and improving cash flow, Gorilla Technology carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), indicating solid growth potential despite a valuation higher than its industry average.
