title: Gold Beats AI? This Miner Just Crushed Palantir In 2025
summary: In 2025, gold miner Newmont Corp. (NYSE: NEM) has surprisingly outperformed AI darling Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR), with Newmont up 105% year-to-date compared to Palantir's 104% gain. This performance highlights a shift towards value and hard assets, as Newmont trades at a significantly lower forward P/E ratio than Palantir, attracting substantial inflows into gold-linked ETFs. Analysts suggest this gold cycle is still in its early stages, with significant upside potential for miners.

title: Palantir Co-Founder Joe Lonsdale Says 'Very Weird' That Trump White House Took 10% Intel Stake, Calls It 'Cronyism In Some Form'
summary: Joe Lonsdale, co-founder of Palantir Technologies, expressed discomfort with the federal government's decision to take an equity stake in Intel Corp., labeling it "cronyism." This move, part of the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act and Secure Enclave program, has drawn criticism from some politicians and economists who warn against market distortions and unconstitutional actions. However, some, like Sen. Bernie Sanders, support the idea of taxpayers getting something in return for corporate welfare.

title: $251 Million Capital Raise: Dianthus Therapeutics Secures Major Funding for Autoimmune Drug Development
summary: Dianthus Therapeutics (NASDAQ: DNTH) has announced the pricing of an upsized public offering, raising approximately $251 million in gross proceeds. This funding, from the sale of common stock and pre-funded warrants at $33.00 per share, will be directed towards advancing the company's preclinical and clinical development activities, particularly for its antibody complement therapeutics targeting severe autoimmune diseases. The offering, which saw a positive market reaction with DNTH gaining 6.55%, is expected to close on September 11, 2025.

title: 10 Top AI Stocks to Buy Now
summary: This article identifies 10 top AI stocks, ranging from speculative microcaps to established giants, poised to benefit from the growing $286 billion AI data center chip market. It highlights companies involved in autonomous robotics, nuclear microreactors for AI power, photonic computing, custom chip manufacturing, power efficiency, GPU cloud services, data management, enterprise AI platforms, chip foundry, and critical lithography equipment. Investors are advised to size positions according to risk tolerance, noting the diverse risk/reward profiles across the listed companies.

title: ProShares Expands Lineup with ETFs Targeting 2x Daily Returns of Coinbase, Nvidia, Palantir, and Tesla
summary: ProShares has expanded its product lineup with new single stock ETFs designed to target 2x daily returns of Coinbase (COIA), NVIDIA (NVDB), Palantir (PLTA), and Tesla (TSLI). These new funds allow investors to magnify the daily returns of these transformative companies. ProShares, as the world's largest provider of geared ETFs, aims to provide strategic and tactical opportunities for investors.

title: Lumen Technologies (LUMN) Unveils Wavelength RapidRoutes For Fast Enterprise Connectivity
summary: Lumen Technologies (LUMN) has launched Wavelength RapidRoutes to enhance enterprise connectivity, alongside partnerships with Palantir for AI integration and Pac-12 Enterprises for sports broadcasting. Despite a 50% stock price increase driven by market highs and AI demand, Lumen's revenue is projected to decline, and its stock trades slightly above analyst targets, reflecting market skepticism. The company aims for margin expansion and incremental revenue through strategic refocus and AI-driven infrastructure contracts, but faces challenges from legacy revenue declines and competitive pressures.

title: Cboe introduces futures and options on the “Magnificent 10” Index
summary: Cboe Global Markets is launching futures and options tied to its new "Magnificent 10" Index in Q4 2025, directly competing with CME Group in the derivatives market for major tech stocks. This index includes the "Mag7" along with Broadcom, Palantir Technologies, and Advanced Micro Devices, collectively representing about 38% of the S&P 500 Index's market value. Additionally, Cboe plans to introduce Bitcoin and Ether Continuous Futures on November 10, 2025, offering U.S. traders regulated perpetual-style futures exposure to cryptocurrencies.

title: PLTA Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:PLTA
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the ProShares Ultra PLTR (PLTA) ETF, which offers 2x leveraged daily returns for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR). It highlights key financial metrics such as Assets Under Management ($6.18M), Expense Ratio (0.95%), and a dividend yield of 4.61%. The article also emphasizes that PLTA is a tactical tool meant for short-term holding due to the effects of daily compounding.

title: Alex Karp Slams Modern Progressivism As Broken, Warns It Keeps Working Class Poor With Failing Policies On Borders, Drugs, Crime
summary: Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) CEO Alex Karp has criticized modern progressivism, stating that it has abandoned the working class and promotes policies that worsen poverty, crime, and drug addiction. Karp advocates for large-scale vocational training, especially in AI, and stricter border enforcement to help the working class. He also dismissed claims of Palantir spying on U.S. citizens and warned about China exploiting American vulnerabilities.

title: Leveraged ETFs: Single-Stock Surge
summary: 2025 has seen a significant surge in leveraged ETFs, primarily single-stock ETFs, with approximately 25% of all funds launched this year falling into this category. This shift from index-based leveraged products reflects investor demand for targeted, short-term tools around specific companies and catalysts, especially within the technology sector and "tech-adjacent" industries. The article highlights new products tied to popular stocks, IPOs, and cryptocurrencies, emphasizing their use as trading instruments rather than core holdings.

title: ETF Talk: Spread Your Risk with This ETF
summary: The article discusses the First Trust Technology Alphadex Fund (FXL) as an ETF alternative for investors seeking technology sector exposure with reduced risk. Unlike many tech ETFs heavily concentrated in a few mega-cap companies, FXL uses a smart-beta approach to offer broader diversification across large- and mid-cap U.S. technology firms, mitigating overconcentration risk. The fund’s strategy involves selecting and weighting holdings based on growth and value metrics, emphasizing favorable fundamentals and spreading investments across a wider range of companies.

title: Seagate Technology Leads S&P 500: What’s Behind Its 120% Gain?
summary: Seagate Technology (NASDAQ: STX) has seen a remarkable 120% gain in 2025, making it the S&P 500's top performer. This surge is primarily attributed to its Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording (HAMR) HDDs, which are crucial for AI data centers due to their unprecedented storage capacity. Despite strong earnings and bullish analyst ratings, concerns about valuation and dependence on the AI industry's CapEx spending persist.

title: Gladstone Capital Corporation $GLAD Shares Sold by Trexquant Investment LP
summary: Trexquant Investment LP significantly reduced its stake in Gladstone Capital (NASDAQ:GLAD) by 43.1%, now holding 26,166 shares valued at $719,000. Despite meeting EPS expectations of $0.50, the company's revenue of $21.66 million missed analyst estimates. Gladstone Capital recently declared a monthly dividend of $0.165 per share, offering an annualized yield of 7.6%.

title: 5 Safe, Inexpensive Ways to Invest in AI
summary: The global AI market is projected to experience significant growth, from $137 billion in 2022 to over $1.81 trillion by 2030, offering substantial investment opportunities. For investors intimidated by individual stock picking, Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) provide a safer and more inexpensive way to gain diversified exposure to the booming AI sector, often with the added benefit of collecting dividends. This article highlights five specific AI-focused ETFs—Global X Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF (AIQ), Global X Robotics and Artificial Intelligence ETF (BOTZ), Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT), iShares Future AI and Tech ETF (ARTY), and Invesco AI and Nex Gen Software ETF (IGPT)—as accessible entry points into AI investment.

title: Stat Lauren Friedman sold Palantir (PLTR) shares worth $3.48m
summary: Stat Lauren Elaina Friedman, a director at Palantir Technologies Inc. (NYSE:PLTR), sold shares worth over $3.48 million on September 11, 2025. The sales involved Class A Common Stock at prices ranging from $164.2626 to $167.25, executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The shares were sold amidst significant stock momentum, with PLTR up 371% over the past year and valued at $405.79 billion.

title: KindlyMD (NASDAQ:NAKA) Stock Price Down 33.4% - Time to Sell?
summary: Shares of KindlyMD (NASDAQ:NAKA) dropped 33.4% to $5.38, with trading volume significantly reduced. Despite this, Maxim Group initiated coverage with a "buy" rating and an $8.00 price target. The company reported a quarterly loss of ($0.34) per share and a negative net margin of 244%.

title: Bullish Surges As Crypto IPO Window Opens
summary: Peter Thiel's backing of Bullish, a Cayman Islands-based digital-asset platform, led to a successful $1.15 billion IPO. The stock more than doubled on its debut, signaling strong investor confidence and opening the "IPO window" for other major crypto companies like Circle, eToro, and Kraken, which are also preparing for public listings. The success of Bullish highlights the growing mainstream adoption and investor interest in digital asset companies.

title: PLTY: Is The Double-Digit Distribution Worth Capping Your Palantir Upside? (PLTY)
summary: The YieldMax PLTR Option Income Strategy ETF (PLTY) sells covered calls on synthetic Palantir stock, offering high distributions but limiting both upside and downside potential compared to owning PLTR directly. While Palantir shows near-term growth, PLTY's call-selling strategy underperforms during strong rallies, and implied volatility is currently low, making call selling less risky but less optimal for maximizing returns. The author rates PLTY a hold, suggesting it might be suitable for income-focused investors with partial PLTR exposure, but better risk-adjusted returns could be found elsewhere.

title: Leidos Holdings, Inc. (LDOS): A Bull Case Theory
summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Leidos Holdings, Inc. (LDOS), a mission services and systems integrator for the U.S. government. The thesis highlights Leidos' predictable cash flows, significant backlog, and potential for a 25-30% upside, contrasting it with Palantir (PLTR)'s higher-risk, product-heavy model. While acknowledging Leidos' stability, the article also points to other AI stocks with potentially higher returns.

title: Lear taps Palantir software for manufacturing operations
summary: Lear Corp. announced a five-year partnership with Palantir Technologies to implement Palantir’s “Warp Speed” manufacturing operating system and Artificial Intelligence Platform across its global operations. This collaboration aims to accelerate industrial automation and streamline manufacturing processes, building on Lear's existing digitization efforts that have already saved over $30 million in the first half of 2025. The AI-powered software will manage various aspects of Lear's business, including quality, supply chain, procurement, manufacturing, finance, and design.

title: Lear taps Palantir software for manufacturing operations
summary: Lear Corp. has partnered with Palantir for a five-year agreement to implement Palantir’s “Warp Speed” manufacturing operating system and Artificial Intelligence Platform across its global manufacturing operations. This collaboration builds on their 2023 work to digitize manufacturing, with Palantir's AI-powered software aiding in streamlining quality, supply chain, procurement, manufacturing, finance, and design, and has already helped Lear's IDEA program save over $30 million in the first half of 2025.

title: Palantir's Defense Dominance Faces First Real Threat—And It's Already Winning
summary: Salesforce's new national security division, Missionforce, is posing a significant challenge to Palantir's long-held dominance in government AI software. Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff announced a win against Palantir for a U.S. Army deal, suggesting that Palantir's premium pricing model might be under threat as agencies consider more cost-effective alternatives. While Palantir maintains a high market cap due to the exclusivity of its AI solutions, Salesforce's aggressive entry and existing government contracts could intensify competition for government AI dollars.

title: Defiance ETFs Unveils $ETHI: First-Ever Leveraged Ethereum ETF with Weekly Income
summary: Defiance ETFs has launched $ETHI, the Defiance Leveraged Long + Income Ethereum ETF, designed to provide retail investors with leveraged exposure to ether-linked ETPs and weekly income through a credit call spread strategy. This ETF aims for 150% to 200% of the daily performance of U.S.-listed ether ETPs while generating consistent cash flow. It offers a regulated structure for Ethereum-linked growth despite not investing directly in ether or digital assets.

title: Swipe Right For Surveillance
summary: This article highlights how the Match Group, a major online dating company, is now led by a former Palantir board member who views AI as a beneficial tool for users. It also touches on broader issues of corporate consolidation, privacy, and censorship, citing specific examples like ABC's suspension of Jimmy Kimmel's show amidst Disney's merger activities and a health data giant's new terms of service. The piece suggests a convergence of surveillance technologies, data exploitation, and corporate power in various sectors.

title: MoneyHero Group Reports Second Quarter 2025 Results
summary: MoneyHero Group announced its Q2 2025 financial results, achieving profitability with a net income of US$0.2 million, a significant improvement from the previous year's net loss. The company's Adjusted EBITDA loss dramatically improved by 79% year-over-year, supported by a stronger revenue mix focusing on higher-margin insurance and wealth verticals, which now comprise 27% of revenue. Strategic investments in AI and partnerships are driving efficiency and growth, with continued emphasis on expanding these high-margin segments and achieving positive Adjusted EBITDA in late 2025.

title: GE Vernova rode the AI boom to the top of the stock market. Why Wall Street believes the rally isn’t over
summary: GE Vernova, a spin-off of General Electric's energy businesses, has seen its stock quadruple since its debut, largely driven by surging electricity demand from AI data centers. Analysts remain bullish on the company, expecting continued growth due to robust demand for its gas turbines and grid equipment, and future opportunities in nuclear power, despite struggles in its wind turbine business. GE Vernova projects significant revenue growth and increased cash reserves, positioning it to capitalize on the increasing power needs of the AI industry.

title: Lear's bold step into lights-out manufacturing in Michigan
summary: Lear Corporation is launching an advanced manufacturing and integration center in Rochester Hills, Michigan, demonstrating "lights-out manufacturing" with 100% automated production for thermal comfort systems. This initiative represents a significant step in the company's decade-long automation journey, aiming for cost efficiencies, scalability, and resilience amidst global economic pressures. The new center will also showcase Lear's expanded partnership with Palantir Technologies for advanced digital integration and AI in its manufacturing processes.

title: Tilt raises $7.1M, launches DIY indexing platform to build the future of wealth management
summary: Tilt, a Miami-based startup, has secured $7.1 million in a seed round led by Portage and Lerer Hippeau to launch its DIY indexing platform. The platform allows individuals to create personalized index funds with features like direct indexing and automated tax-loss harvesting, offering more control than traditional ETFs. Initially targeting registered investment advisors and wealth management firms, Tilt aims to modernize wealth management by enabling custom, theme-based portfolios.

title: KLAS ranks key end-to-end RCM outsourcing vendors
summary: KLAS has published a report ranking end-to-end Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) outsourcing vendors based on client satisfaction. Ensemble achieved the highest overall performance score, followed by Guidehouse, while Optum and R1 RCM received lower satisfaction ratings due to various client concerns. The report also highlights key factors healthcare organizations consider when selecting an RCM outsourcing partner, including reputation, contract terms, partnership potential, and technology.

title: Stat Lauren Friedman’s spouse sells Palantir shares worth $1.59m
summary: The spouse of Palantir Director Stat Lauren Elaina Friedman sold 9,000 shares of Palantir Technologies Inc. (NYSE:PLTR) for $1.59 million, at a price of $177.60 per share. This transaction occurred as PLTR trades near its 52-week high and is considered overvalued by InvestingPro analysis, despite recent announcements of significant military contracts and maintained "Neutral" ratings from Mizuho and UBS. Following the sale, Friedman's spouse directly owns 62,887 shares, with additional indirect holdings.

title: Vistra Stock Falls from Record After Bear Note
summary: Vistra Corp (NYSE: VST) stock fell 5.7% after Jefferies downgraded the Texas-based utility to "hold" from "buy" and cut its price target to $230 from $241, following the stock's significant rise. Despite the downgrade and an "overbought" Relative Strength Index, the stock is still up 9.3% for the month. Options traders showed high call volume before the dip, with low implied volatility expectations suggesting options could be a good way to play the stock's next move.

title: AutoZone Stock Gaps Lower After Quarterly Profit Miss
summary: AutoZone (NYSE: AZO) shares gapped lower by 2.9% after the automotive parts retailer missed fiscal fourth-quarter earnings expectations, despite beating revenue estimates and reporting strong same-store sales growth. The stock is pulling back from its September 11 all-time high of $4,388.11, extending below its 20-day moving average, though it still holds a 33.4% year-over-year gain. Analyst sentiment remains largely bullish, with 23 out of 27 firms rating it a "buy" or better.

title: Boeing Defense, Space & Security Partners with Palantir to Accelerate AI Adoption Across Defense, Classified Programs
summary: Boeing Defense, Space & Security (BDS) has announced a partnership with Palantir to integrate AI systems and software across its factories and programs. This collaboration will leverage Palantir's Foundry platform to standardize data analytics and insights, aiming to accelerate the delivery of critical products and services to military operators. The partnership also includes classified efforts to support sensitive missions for military customers.

title: Evaxion Stock Climbs Amid Biotech Surge
summary: Evaxion A/S (EVAX) stock climbed by over 33% amid a biotech surge, driven by promising news and investor confidence. The article details recent financial performance, including a modest revenue and a high price-to-sales ratio, and highlights the company's impactful AI-driven vaccine innovations. Despite profitability challenges, Evaxion's strategic plans and global showcases of its personalized cancer vaccines are generating significant investor excitement and speculation.

title: Here’s How Not Holding Palantir Technologies (PLTR) Impacted Columbia Seligman Global Technology Fund’s Performance
summary: The article discusses how the Columbia Seligman Global Technology Fund's performance was impacted by not holding Palantir Technologies (PLTR) during Q2 2025. Despite the fund's 19.52% return, it underperformed the MSCI World Information Technology Index, partly due to Palantir's significant stock price increase from securing government contracts. The fund noted that Palantir is not among its top picks, favoring other AI stocks with potentially higher returns.

title: GraniteShares Announces Weekly Distributions for its YieldBOOST ETFs: COYY, TSYY, NVYY, XBTY, AMYY, TQQY, AZYY, YSPY, MTYY and PLYY
summary: GraniteShares has announced the weekly distributions for its YieldBOOST ETFs, including COYY, TSYY, NVYY, XBTY, AMYY, TQQY, AZYY, YSPY, MTYY, and PLYY, with payouts occurring on September 30, 2025. The announcement includes detailed distribution rates, 30-Day SEC Yields, and ex-dates for each ETF. Investors are advised that distributions are not guaranteed and past performance does not indicate future results.

title: 1 High-Flying Stock to Own for Decades and 2 We Turn Down
summary: This article identifies one high-flying stock, Palantir Technologies (PLTR), as a strong buy due to its contract wins, rapid customer acquisition recovery, and strong free cash flow margin. Conversely, it advises selling Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM) and Advanced Energy (AEIS) because of declining revenues, eroding profitability, and other fundamental weaknesses. The report emphasizes balancing price with quality and highlights StockStory's success in identifying high-quality stocks.

title: ETFs Investing in Palantir Technologies Inc. Class A Stocks
summary: This article lists various Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) that hold Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) Class A stocks. It provides a detailed table including each fund's market value, weight of PLTR in the fund, issuer, management style, focus, expense ratio, assets under management (AUM), price, change, relative volume, and 3-year NAV total return. The ETFs cover a range of investment strategies, from broad market and large-cap funds to specific technology and thematic ETFs.

title: ETF investissant dans des actions Palantir Technologies Inc. Class A
summary: This article provides a detailed list of Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) that hold Palantir Technologies Inc. Class A shares, classified by market value. It includes various financial metrics for each ETF such as market value, weight, issuer, management style, expense ratio, AUM, price, percentage change, and total NAV return over three years. The information aims to assist investors looking for lower-risk opportunities in Palantir shares through diversified funds.

title: Prediction: 1 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Will Be Worth More Than Nvidia and Palantir Combined by 2030
summary: Alphabet, despite its current valuation being lower than the combined market caps of Nvidia and Palantir, is predicted to surpass them by 2030 due to its strong growth opportunities in AI-enhanced search, cloud computing, and autonomous driving via Waymo. While Nvidia faces risks from custom AI chips and Palantir's valuation is considered extremely high, Alphabet's diversified AI integration and relatively cheaper stock price position it for significant future growth. The article argues that Alphabet's vertically integrated cloud business and its consumer-facing AI advancements make it a strong contender for becoming the world's largest company.

title: Downgrade, C-Suite Split Send Spotify Stock Lower
summary: Spotify Technology SA (NYSE:SPOT) shares dropped 2.8% after CEO Daniel Ek announced his resignation, to be replaced by co-CEOs Alex Norström and Gustav Söderström, effective January 1. Goldman Sachs subsequently downgraded SPOT to "neutral" from "buy," trimming its price target to $765 from $770, citing that much of the company's value is already priced in. Despite today's decline, Spotify stock remains up 55% for 2025, finding support at its 100-day moving average, with options traders showing increased interest in put options.

title: LendingClub Stock Posing Attractive Entry Point for Bulls
summary: LendingClub (NYSE: LC) stock is experiencing a sharp decline but presents a historically bullish entry point for investors. Despite recent losses, the stock is up 32.6% year-over-year and is flashing a historically reliable signal after pulling back to its 50-day moving average. The company also shows low implied volatility and significant short interest, suggesting potential for a strong bounce.

title: Dronemaker DraganFly’s Shares Soar 13.6% After Contract With U.S. Army: Is There More Lift to Come?
summary: Draganfly's shares surged 13.6% after securing a contract with the U.S. Army to supply Flex FPV drone systems, highlighting the company's growing role in defense and its strategic positioning for future growth. The agreement includes drone delivery, localized manufacturing, personnel training, and logistics, aligning with the U.S. Army's evolving tactical needs and the Department of Defense's push for domestic drone production. With significant year-over-year stock growth and expanding commercial opportunities beyond military contracts, Draganfly appears well-positioned for continued market performance.

title: UDR Appoints Richard B. Clark to Board
summary: UDR, Inc. has appointed Richard B. Clark to its Board of Directors as part of a long-term succession plan, expanding the board to nine members. Clark is a seasoned real estate executive with extensive experience in capital markets and property management. He will serve on the Nominating and Governance Committee and the Audit and Risk Management Committee, and currently, analyst sentiment for UDR stock is a Hold with a $40.00 price target.

This shift indicates a new phase in the AI boom, broadening the range of influential large-cap companies.

title: Palantir Technologies Director Sells 20,000 Shares
summary: Alexander D. Moore, a Director at Palantir Technologies, sold 20,000 shares of Class A Common Stock on October 1, 2025, for a total of $3,680,224. The shares were sold at weighted average prices ranging from $180.4306 to $186.27. Following this transaction, Moore directly owns 1,252,978 shares of Palantir Technologies.

title: 59% YTD Return: NUKZ — VettaFi Acquires Nuclear Index Suite, Expands 1,250-Index Platform
summary: VettaFi has acquired a suite of three nuclear indices, including the Range Nuclear Renaissance Index (NUKZ), from Range Fund Holdings and North Shore Indices, expanding its platform to over 1,250 indexes. The NUKZ ETF has demonstrated strong performance with a 59% year-to-date return and over $630 million in assets, driven by increasing AI-related electricity demand and pro-nuclear policies. This acquisition strengthens VettaFi's position in offering specialized investment exposure to long-term megatrends.

title: Schwab Trading Activity Index™: STAX Score Keeps Rising in September
summary: The Schwab Trading Activity Index (STAX) increased to 46.12 in September, up from 43.69 in August, indicating that Schwab clients were net buyers of equities and branching out beyond traditional names like the "Magnificent Seven." This rise in activity outpaced the S&P 500 index, driven by factors such as a mid-month rate cut announcement, lighter inflation data, and resilient retail sales. Clients showed increased interest in higher-volatility, AI-adjacent stocks, with NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA) and Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) among the popular names bought, while Tesla Inc. (TSLA) and Apple Inc. (AAPL) were net sold.

title: OneMedNet selects Palantir to power healthcare data analytics platform
summary: OneMedNet Corporation (NASDAQ: ONMD) has partnered with Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) to integrate Palantir's AI Platform with OneMedNet's healthcare data network. This collaboration aims to enhance data analytics capabilities for life sciences, medical device, and research organizations, despite OneMedNet facing financial challenges like declining revenue and negative gross margins. The partnership will utilize Palantir's technology to deliver anonymized, regulatory-grade clinical data, featuring conversational search and integration with healthcare standards, while OneMedNet's AI algorithms ensure data de-identification and regulatory compliance.

title: Ações da OneMedNet disparam, Palantir sobe em parceria plurianual de IA na área da saúde
summary: As ações da OneMedNet (NASDAQ:ONMD) e da Palantir Technologies (NYSE:PLTR) subiram após anunciarem uma parceria plurianual para transformar a inteligência artificial e análise de dados do mundo real (RWD) na área da saúde. A colaboração combinará a Plataforma de IA da Palantir com a rede iRWD™ da OneMedNet, visando acelerar a inovação em diagnósticos e tratamentos. Esta parceria endereça um mercado de IA na saúde estimado em US$ 868 bilhões até 2030, prometendo avanços tecnológicos como descoberta de dados mais rápida e maior precisão.

title: Equillium announces leadership changes as it shifts focus to EQ504
summary: Equillium, Inc. announced leadership changes and a refocusing of its pipeline on EQ504, a novel aryl hydrocarbon receptor modulator. Stephen Connelly was promoted to President and Chief Scientific Officer, while Daniel M. Bradbury transitioned to Chairman of the Board, and Bruce Steel remained CEO. The company also terminated its collaboration with Biocon for itolizumab and is now concentrating on EQ504 for inflammatory diseases.

title: 2 Defense Stocks Riding 2025’s Massive Momentum Wave
summary: This article analyzes two defense stocks, Kratos Defense and Security Solutions (KTOS) and Karman (KRMN), which have seen significant momentum-driven gains of over 100% in 2025. Despite their high forward P/E ratios and some bearish signals like overbought RSI readings and analyst price targets implying downside, the article suggests that their rallies could continue, referencing Palantir Technologies as an example of sustained high-valuation growth. Investors are cautioned about the substantial risk and the need for excellent execution in upcoming earnings reports to justify current valuations.

title: 2 Defense Stocks Riding 2025’s Massive Momentum Wave
summary: This article discusses two defense stocks, Kratos Defense and Security Solutions (KTOS) and Karman (KRMN), that have experienced significant momentum-driven rallies in 2025, with their shares soaring over 100%. Despite high forward P/E ratios and some bearish signals like high RSIs and analyst downside targets, the article suggests these stocks could continue rising, drawing parallels to Palantir Technologies' own surge. However, it also emphasizes the substantial risk involved and the need for strong execution in upcoming earnings reports to justify their current valuations.

title: Stat Lauren Friedman of Palantir sells $149,112 in stock
summary: Stat Lauren Friedman, a Director at Palantir Technologies Inc., sold 800 shares of Class A Common Stock for a total of $149,112. The transaction was executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, with PLTR trading near its 52-week high after a 340% return over the past year. Friedman still holds a significant number of shares directly and indirectly.

title: Roundhill brings back MEME ETF as retail traders chase risk again
summary: Roundhill Investments is relaunching its MEME ETF in 2025, two years after its initial closure. The new version is actively managed, tracking retail sentiment on social media and rebalancing weekly to capitalize on volatile stocks. This relaunch signifies a renewed appetite for speculative trading among retail investors, particularly amidst a booming market and under new economic policies.

title: C3.ai’s Fall from Grace: A Failing Stock in the AI Revolution - AOL.com
summary: C3.ai (AI) stock plummeted after disappointing fiscal Q1 2026 results and the announcement of CEO Thomas M. Siebel's departure due to health issues. Despite being in the AI sector, the company has struggled to convert enthusiasm into sales or profits, failing to keep pace with competitors like Nvidia and Palantir. This performance, coupled with leadership turmoil and withdrawn guidance, paints a grim picture for its future, leading analysts to advise selling the stock.

title: Why Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), Tempus AI (TEM) and Teladoc (TDOC) Are Up Big Today
summary: Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), Tempus AI (TEM), and Teladoc (TDOC) stocks saw significant gains despite general market downturns. Hims & Hers is benefiting from its upcoming Super Bowl commercial, which has generated controversy, while Tempus AI is riding a hot streak fueled by recent call option purchases, even as an insider sells shares. Teladoc's stock climb is attributed to a positive report from short-seller Citron Research, highlighting cost-cutting and strong free cash flow.

title: BigBear.ai vs. SoundHound AI: What's the Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock to Buy Today? - AOL.com
summary: This article compares two AI stocks, BigBear.ai (BBAI) and SoundHound AI (SOUN), to determine which is a better buy. BigBear.ai is a data analytics company with government contracts, well-funded operations, and promising future opportunities, despite recent sales volatility. SoundHound AI is a voice AI company that significantly boosted its revenue through an acquisition and has Nvidia's investment, but carries higher cash burn. The author ultimately recommends BigBear.ai due to its financial stability and government contracts.

title: Venture Funding Roars Back in Q3, Thanks to AI's Billion-Dollar Boom
summary: Global venture capital funding saw a significant rebound in Q3 2025, reaching $97 billion, a 38% year-over-year increase, primarily driven by massive investments in Artificial Intelligence companies. AI received approximately 46% of all Q3 funding, with a substantial portion going to Anthropic. This marks the fourth consecutive quarter of year-over-year growth in venture funding and a rise in initial public offerings for venture-backed companies.

title: Want $1 Million in Retirement? 2 Simple Index Funds to Buy and Hold for Decades. - AOL.com
summary: This article suggests two index funds, the Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI) and the Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT), as simple investment vehicles to potentially reach $1 million in retirement over several decades. It provides examples of monthly contributions and initial investments needed to achieve this goal, highlighting the diversified approach of VTI and the higher potential returns (and risk) of VGT.

title: Prediction: 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks That Will Be Worth More Than Palantir by the End of 2026
summary: This article predicts that two AI stocks, Alibaba Group (BABA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), will surpass Palantir Technologies (PLTR) in market capitalization by the end of 2026. title: Red Cat Shares Jump On Palantir-Powered Drone Test Success
summary: Red Cat Holdings' shares surged after a successful flight test of its Black Widow drone, equipped with Palantir Technologies' Visual Navigation (VNav) Software. The test demonstrated the drone's ability to navigate under GPS-denied conditions, a critical capability for military applications. This achievement marks a significant step for Red Cat's evolution into a defense technology platform, with expected margin expansion and revenue growth.

title: 4 stocks to watch on Monday: KKR, PLTR, KDP, LLY
summary: Stock index futures were higher on Monday as investors geared up for a week filled with tech earnings, a Federal Reserve meeting, and updates on U.S.-China trade relations. This article highlights four specific stocks—KKR, PLTR, KDP, and LLY—that investors should watch on this day.

title: Sysco Corp. (SYY) Tops Q1 EPS by 3c, Beats on Revenue
summary: Sysco Corp. (SYY) announced strong financial results for its first quarter, reporting an EPS of $1.15, surpassing analyst estimates by $0.03. The company also exceeded revenue expectations, bringing in $21.1 billion against a consensus of $21.07 billion. This performance signals a positive start to the fiscal year for Sysco.

title: Palantir e Nvidia juntam-se no desenvolvimento de IA
summary: Palantir and Nvidia have formed a strategic partnership to integrate Nvidia's advanced computing technologies with Palantir's AI platform to create sophisticated AI agents and automate business processes. The collaboration aims to provide a comprehensive technology stack for businesses, with Lowe's already utilizing the solution to optimize its supply chain logistics. This partnership leverages Nvidia's hardware leadership and Palantir's software expertise to set a new standard for operational AI applications across various sectors.

title: Broadcom, IBD Stock Of The Day, Breaks Out Past Buy Point On AI Activity
summary: Broadcom, IBD's Stock Of The Day, has broken out past its buy point, driven by AI activity. The stock, trading under ticker AVGO, is up 10% this month and 57% year to date, showcasing strong performance despite near-term risks associated with tech giants' capital expenditure guidance. Broadcom's robust Composite Rating of 99/99 and its "Cup with Handle" chart pattern indicate a positive outlook for investors.

title: Palantir and Nvidia Join Forces in Advanced Artificial Intelligence Development
summary: Palantir and Nvidia have formed a strategic partnership to integrate Nvidia's advanced computing technologies with Palantir's AI platform, aiming to create a comprehensive technology stack for enterprises. This collaboration will enable the development of advanced AI agents, process automation, and enhanced analytical capabilities. Lowe's is already utilizing this solution to optimize its supply chain logistics, showcasing a practical application of the integrated AI hardware and software.

title: Tariffs Are Back on the Menu: This Magnificent BlackRock ETF Could Help Protect Your Portfolio
summary: Tariffs inherently foster domestic manufacturing, though they can hurt economic growth by increasing costs for consumers, creating a challenge for investors. This article suggests that BlackRock's iShares U.S. Tech Independence Focused ETF (IETC) can help investors protect their portfolios from the adverse effects of tariffs. The IETC ETF invests in American technology companies that produce more of their products or intellectual property domestically, making them less susceptible to trade policy fluctuations.

title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Palantir Technologies Inc. - PLTR
summary: Pomerantz LLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:PLTR) regarding possible securities fraud or unlawful business practices. This investigation follows media reports on October 3, 2025, that an internal U.S. Army memo described Palantir's NGC2 platform as "very high risk" due to security vulnerabilities, causing Palantir's stock price to fall by 7.47%.

title: Nvidia becomes the world's first $5 trillion company, buoyed by AI boom
summary: Nvidia has become the world's first publicly listed company to reach a $5 trillion market valuation, driven by strong investor confidence in the growth of artificial intelligence. Its shares have surged due to high demand for its GPUs used in AI applications and gaming, alongside new product rollouts and partnerships. While some voice concerns about an AI bubble, analysts suggest the current tech rally differs from past crashes due to companies' strong financial positions.

title: Nvidia becomes first company to close above $5 trillion market cap
summary: Nvidia made history by becoming the first company to close above a $5 trillion market capitalization, driven by optimism around potential sales in China following comments from President Trump and a series of new partnerships and announcements. The company's stock has surged over 50% this year, fueled by strong demand for its AI chips from major tech companies and its own strategic investments, despite increasing competition in the AI chip market.

title: Analysts Conflicted on These Technology Names: Snowflake (SNOW) and Lyft (LYFT)
summary: This article highlights analyst ratings for Snowflake (SNOW) and Lyft (LYFT) in the technology sector. Mizuho Securities maintained a Buy rating for Snowflake with a $325 price target, while Oppenheimer also reiterated a Buy rating. For Lyft, Mizuho Securities maintained a Hold rating with a $21 price target, which was echoed by Barclays' Hold rating.

title: DRNZ vs JEDI: Two Takes on the Drone Trade
summary: The article contrasts two distinct drone-focused ETFs: the REX Drone ETF (DRNZ) and the Defiance Drone & Modern Warfare ETF (JEDI). DRNZ, launched recently, offers a pure-play exposure to the drone industry across various sectors, tracking the VettaFi Drone Index. JEDI, launched earlier, provides a broader investment in modern warfare technologies, with drones being only one component, tracking the BITA Drone & Modern Warfare Select Index.

title: Errors of Omission in Mid-Cap Growth Funds
summary: Mid-cap growth funds underperformed their benchmark in the past year due to increased index concentration and the dilemma managers faced regarding high-flying stocks like AppLovin and Palantir. Many managers chose to stick to their investment philosophies, leading to varied relative performances. The article discusses potential solutions like more frequent index reconstitutions or adopting alternative benchmarks like the Morningstar US Mid Cap Growth Broad Index.

title: Samsung building facility with 50,000 Nvidia GPUs to automate chip manufacturing
summary: Samsung plans to build an "AI Megafactory" equipped with 50,000 Nvidia GPUs to enhance its chip manufacturing processes for mobile devices and robots. This collaboration highlights Nvidia's continued dominance in AI chip technology and strengthens its partnership with Samsung, which also acts as a key supplier for Nvidia's high-bandwidth memory. The initiative underscores the increasing integration of AI into complex manufacturing and the strategic alliances forming within the semiconductor industry.

title: Can Investor Caution Around AI Shift the Software Narrative for Roper Technologies (ROP)?
summary: Roper Technologies reported strong Q3 results and authorized a US$3 billion share repurchase, yet investor caution persists due to AI uncertainty impacting its software business and M&A integration risks. RBC Capital Markets downgraded the stock citing these AI concerns and near-term headwinds, shifting focus to AI adoption as a key catalyst and risk for Roper's future performance. The company forecasts significant revenue and earnings growth by 2028, with a fair value indicating a 41% upside despite varied community estimates.

The partnership is expected to drive efficiency, reduce costs, and improve regulatory compliance in real-world evidence generation, particularly benefiting complex fields like precision medicine.
